cells.title: Overview
cells.source: This notebook presents a rentier-split extension of the Godley and Lavoie DIS model. It preserves the original inventory, pricing, bank credit, and wealth dynamics, while splitting households into workers and rentiers so that wages, profits, deposit interest, consumption, and wealth accumulation can be tracked separately.

cells.title: DIS rentier balance sheet
cells.description: Balance-sheet matrix for the rentier-split DIS model, separating worker and rentier deposit holdings while keeping firms, inventories, and banks explicit.
cells.note: This makes wealth distribution visible: workers and rentiers hold distinct money stocks, while bank liabilities and firm borrowing remain stock-flow consistent.

cells.title: DIS rentier transactions-flow matrix
cells.description: Transactions-flow matrix for the rentier-split DIS model, separating wages, profits, deposit interest, consumption, and wealth accumulation between workers and rentiers.
cells.note: Use this to show that wages primarily fund worker consumption while profits, bank profits, and deposit interest accrue to rentiers, making distributional feedbacks visible in the accounting.

cells.title: DIS rentier transaction flow sequence
cells.description: Sequence view of the rentier-split DIS transactions-flow matrix, showing how spending, income, and financial positions propagate through the selected period.

cells.title: DIS rentier equation dependency graph
cells.description: Dependency view of the rentier-split DIS equations, highlighting how expected sales, inventories, pricing, bank credit, wage income, profits, deposit interest, and wealth feed back through the model over time.
cells.note: Use this alongside the transactions-flow matrix to connect the accounting structure with the model's dynamic distributional adjustment mechanisms.
